gallup_top5.definition: “成就”才干主题突出的人大都兢兢业业，锲而不舍。 他们乐于忙忙碌碌并有所作为。
gallup_top5.feature: work hard and possess a great deal of stamina. They take immense satisfaction in being busy and productive.
gallup_top5.application: You likely have an innate intensity and drive that enables you to get a lot done and set the pace for your team. You are dependable — others can count on you to get the job done and reach your goals. Breaking a large project down into manageable tasks likely comes naturally to you. As such, you can help others see big jobs as less daunting. You have stamina — not only do you like to work hard, you derive a lot of energy from working hard; the harder you work, the more capacity you find you have.
gallup_top5.blind_spots: Your Achiever talents push you, so you might tend to push others — sometimes too hard. Because you work hard, you may become frustrated with those whom you perceive as not working as hard as you do; as such, peers may see you as too demanding, inflexible and judgmental of others. Your confidence in your ability to get things done may cause you to jump in and get started on a project before understanding all the details, nuances and demands of the project -- and that can get you into trouble. With your focus on task completion, you might overlook or underestimate the value of people and relationships. If you are a manager or team leader, you may need to remind yourself that managing is not only about getting work done through people, but as importantly it's about getting people done through work. People with strong Achiever talents may get frustrated when others don’t work as hard as they do. Others may see them as too demanding. They need to remember that not everyone has the same high expectations for themselves or is driven to work as hard as they are. Their pressing need to get things done might cause them to take on projects or agree to deadlines before they know everything that’s involved. Before they commit to something, high Achievers should make sure they have the time and resources they need to do it right.

gallup_top5.definition: “伯乐”才干主题突出者善于发现并培养他人的潜能。 他们能够察觉任何微小的进步，并从中得到满足感。
gallup_top5.feature: 欣赏他人潜力并善于发现进步的人，喜悦于细微的成长，激励他人不断进步。
gallup_top5.application: 观察和评估身边的人，发现他们的优点、才能和潜力，注意他们的成就和特长。 与他人建立良好的沟通和合作关系，了解他们的目标和需求，帮助他们发展他们的潜力，并在实现目标 的过程中提供指导和建议。 通过认可和赞赏，激发他人的动力和自信心。表达对他们的信任，并鼓励他们尝试新的挑战和机会。 通过良好的领导实践和积极的影响力，发展自己的领导能力，成为他人的榜样和引导者。 与他人建立导师关系，分享经验和知识，帮助他们发展专业能力和职业发展。
gallup_top5.blind_spots: · 依赖他人的赞许和认可，忽视自身的价值和成就，导致自我价值感受受损。 · 过分专注于他人的潜力和成功，忽略了自己的个人成长和发展，可能导致自身进步的缓慢。 · 对他人的成长和进步寄予过高期望，忽视每个人的独特情况和发展速度，可能带来不必要的压力和失望。

gallup_top5.definition: 分析能力强的人喜欢探究事物的来龙去脉。 他们有能力思考可能影响局面的诸多因素。
gallup_top5.feature: 具有强大分析能力的人喜欢深入探究事物的来龙去脉，能够综合考虑各种因素对局势的影响。
gallup_top5.application: 积极收集相关的信息和数据，利用各种资源，包括书籍、报告、研究和互联网，收集全面的信息。 运用逻辑思维和分析能力，对收集到的数据进行分析和解读，从中获取有价值的见解。 基于分析的结果，提出假设和推理，推导出合理的结论和预测。 在做出决策之前，评估潜在的风险和机会，制定相应的应对策略。 根据分析的结果，制定决策和行动计划。将决策细化为具体的步骤和时间表，确保执行的可行性和有效 性。 实施决策后，密切监测执行的结果 ,定期评估决策的效果，并根据反馈进行调整和改进。
gallup_top5.blind_spots: · 选择性地采集和使用数据，忽视或排除了其他重要的信息和数据源。 · 基于错误或不准确的假设进行分析，导致结论的不准确性和误导性。 · 只关注单一的因素或变量，忽略了问题的复杂性和与其他因素的相互影响。

gallup_top5.definition: 统率力强的人有大将风度。他们运筹帷幄，指挥若定。
gallup_top5.feature: have presence. They can take control of a situation and make decisions.
gallup_top5.application: You have a natural ability to inspire and, as such, your words carry weight. Others often will defer to your personality as much as to your expertise or experience. While you may value time alone, you are not a "loner" — you need people; specifically, you need people to follow you. Because you are focused on others, you can be charming in your efforts to gain followers. This quality makes others want to follow your lead. You have a very protective nature — you will stand up and often intimidate those who would threaten your team at work or the people you love and care about. You do not hide from the truth, no matter how unpleasant that might be. This leads you to say out loud what others may only be thinking. You can become the voice of the voiceless.
gallup_top5.blind_spots: Your Command talents drive you to seek clarity, often through confrontation. Others can see this as intimidating and may even perceive you as a bully. Be careful not to intimidate those you seek to inspire. You are naturally decisive which can cause others to shy away from sharing their insights and ideas. Seek others' points of view before you make a decision. You have an opinion about the way things should be done and are not shy about expressing it. Be careful about imposing your will upon others lest they see you as bossy or arrogant. You are direct and sometimes can offend others without meaning to. Find ways to soften your approach and expand your influence — create an encouraging environment to draw others to follow you. Your decisiveness and presence can be intimidating, whether you intend them to be or not. Keep that in mind, especially when you have to coach others, deliver feedback and set expectations. Because you speak with authority, you might be used to getting the final word. Consider asking others for their input before sharing yours so they have a chance to contribute.

gallup_top5.definition: 公平心强的人深知应平等待人。他们希望有固定的章程以 及清晰的规则和程序，每个人都可以遵循。
gallup_top5.feature: are keenly aware of the need to treat people the same. They crave stable routines and clear rules and procedures that everyone can follow.
gallup_top5.application: Others see you as predictable, and this is a positive observation. Your team knows where they stand with you and that everyone will receive a fair shake. You set clear expectations — for yourself, your team as a whole and each team member. Standards are uniformly defined, and metrics are clear. In the midst of the complexity and rapid change that is ever-present in today's work environment, your Consistency talents bring clarity and understanding to multi-faceted situations. Your ability to help others see what isn't changing can help a team navigate the waters of uncertainty. You give credit where credit is due, which helps your team members recognise their accomplishments and also know that you value the contributions they make.
gallup_top5.blind_spots: Your natural tendency to treat people the same can sometimes fail to recognise and celebrate the uniqueness of each individual. Sometimes those with strong internal Consistency talents can be seen as rigid or inflexible. Be careful that your "the rules apply to everybody" perspective doesn't keep you from considering legitimate exceptions and extenuating circumstances. Sometimes those with Consistency can come across as self-righteous, contending that their rules-based view of the world is the only way to view reality. You may tend to place undue emphasis on the way work gets done rather than focusing on the outcome — overvaluing process over results. You are so committed to treating each person the same that you might forget that no two people are alike. Remember that different things motivate different people, and everyone has their own way of thinking and relating to others. You may see yourself as a guardian of what is right and a champion against special treatment. Realize that others may reject you for assuming that responsibility and will want to bend the rules for the greater good.

成就 / Achiever（执行力 / Executing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“成就”才干主题突出的人大都兢兢业业，锲而不舍。 他们乐于忙忙碌碌并有所作为。

特征 / Feature：work hard and possess a great deal of stamina. They take immense satisfaction in being busy and productive.

应用 / Application：You likely have an innate intensity and drive that enables you to get a lot done and set the pace for your team. You are dependable — others can count on you to get the job done and reach your goals. Breaking a large project down into manageable tasks likely comes naturally to you. As such, you can help others see big jobs as less daunting. You have stamina — not only do you like to work hard, you derive a lot of energy from working hard; the harder you work, the more capacity you find you have.

盲点 / Blind Spots：Your Achiever talents push you, so you might tend to push others — sometimes too hard. Because you work hard, you may become frustrated with those whom you perceive as not working as hard as you do; as such, peers may see you as too demanding, inflexible and judgmental of others. Your confidence in your ability to get things done may cause you to jump in and get started on a project before understanding all the details, nuances and demands of the project -- and that can get you into trouble. With your focus on task completion, you might overlook or underestimate the value of people and relationships. If you are a manager or team leader, you may need to remind yourself that managing is not only about getting work done through people, but as importantly it's about getting people done through work. People with strong Achiever talents may get frustrated when others don’t work as hard as they do. Others may see them as too demanding. They need to remember that not everyone has the same high expectations for themselves or is driven to work as hard as they are. Their pressing need to get things done might cause them to take on projects or agree to deadlines before they know everything that’s involved. Before they commit to something, high Achievers should make sure they have the time and resources they need to do it right.

伯乐 / Developer（关系建立 / Relationship Building）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“伯乐”才干主题突出者善于发现并培养他人的潜能。 他们能够察觉任何微小的进步，并从中得到满足感。

特征 / Feature：欣赏他人潜力并善于发现进步的人，喜悦于细微的成长，激励他人不断进步。

应用 / Application：观察和评估身边的人，发现他们的优点、才能和潜力，注意他们的成就和特长。 与他人建立良好的沟通和合作关系，了解他们的目标和需求，帮助他们发展他们的潜力，并在实现目标 的过程中提供指导和建议。 通过认可和赞赏，激发他人的动力和自信心。表达对他们的信任，并鼓励他们尝试新的挑战和机会。 通过良好的领导实践和积极的影响力，发展自己的领导能力，成为他人的榜样和引导者。 与他人建立导师关系，分享经验和知识，帮助他们发展专业能力和职业发展。

盲点 / Blind Spots：· 依赖他人的赞许和认可，忽视自身的价值和成就，导致自我价值感受受损。 · 过分专注于他人的潜力和成功，忽略了自己的个人成长和发展，可能导致自身进步的缓慢。 · 对他人的成长和进步寄予过高期望，忽视每个人的独特情况和发展速度，可能带来不必要的压力和失望。

分析 / Analytical（战略思维 / Strategic Thinking）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：分析能力强的人喜欢探究事物的来龙去脉。 他们有能力思考可能影响局面的诸多因素。

特征 / Feature：具有强大分析能力的人喜欢深入探究事物的来龙去脉，能够综合考虑各种因素对局势的影响。

应用 / Application：积极收集相关的信息和数据，利用各种资源，包括书籍、报告、研究和互联网，收集全面的信息。 运用逻辑思维和分析能力，对收集到的数据进行分析和解读，从中获取有价值的见解。 基于分析的结果，提出假设和推理，推导出合理的结论和预测。 在做出决策之前，评估潜在的风险和机会，制定相应的应对策略。 根据分析的结果，制定决策和行动计划。将决策细化为具体的步骤和时间表，确保执行的可行性和有效 性。 实施决策后，密切监测执行的结果 ,定期评估决策的效果，并根据反馈进行调整和改进。

盲点 / Blind Spots：· 选择性地采集和使用数据，忽视或排除了其他重要的信息和数据源。 · 基于错误或不准确的假设进行分析，导致结论的不准确性和误导性。 · 只关注单一的因素或变量，忽略了问题的复杂性和与其他因素的相互影响。

统率 / Command（影响力 / Influencing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：统率力强的人有大将风度。他们运筹帷幄，指挥若定。

特征 / Feature：have presence. They can take control of a situation and make decisions.

应用 / Application：You have a natural ability to inspire and, as such, your words carry weight. Others often will defer to your personality as much as to your expertise or experience. While you may value time alone, you are not a "loner" — you need people; specifically, you need people to follow you. Because you are focused on others, you can be charming in your efforts to gain followers. This quality makes others want to follow your lead. You have a very protective nature — you will stand up and often intimidate those who would threaten your team at work or the people you love and care about. You do not hide from the truth, no matter how unpleasant that might be. This leads you to say out loud what others may only be thinking. You can become the voice of the voiceless.

盲点 / Blind Spots：Your Command talents drive you to seek clarity, often through confrontation. Others can see this as intimidating and may even perceive you as a bully. Be careful not to intimidate those you seek to inspire. You are naturally decisive which can cause others to shy away from sharing their insights and ideas. Seek others' points of view before you make a decision. You have an opinion about the way things should be done and are not shy about expressing it. Be careful about imposing your will upon others lest they see you as bossy or arrogant. You are direct and sometimes can offend others without meaning to. Find ways to soften your approach and expand your influence — create an encouraging environment to draw others to follow you. Your decisiveness and presence can be intimidating, whether you intend them to be or not. Keep that in mind, especially when you have to coach others, deliver feedback and set expectations. Because you speak with authority, you might be used to getting the final word. Consider asking others for their input before sharing yours so they have a chance to contribute.

公平 / Consistency（执行力 / Executing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：公平心强的人深知应平等待人。他们希望有固定的章程以 及清晰的规则和程序，每个人都可以遵循。

特征 / Feature：are keenly aware of the need to treat people the same. They crave stable routines and clear rules and procedures that everyone can follow.

应用 / Application：Others see you as predictable, and this is a positive observation. Your team knows where they stand with you and that everyone will receive a fair shake. You set clear expectations — for yourself, your team as a whole and each team member. Standards are uniformly defined, and metrics are clear. In the midst of the complexity and rapid change that is ever-present in today's work environment, your Consistency talents bring clarity and understanding to multi-faceted situations. Your ability to help others see what isn't changing can help a team navigate the waters of uncertainty. You give credit where credit is due, which helps your team members recognise their accomplishments and also know that you value the contributions they make.

盲点 / Blind Spots：Your natural tendency to treat people the same can sometimes fail to recognise and celebrate the uniqueness of each individual. Sometimes those with strong internal Consistency talents can be seen as rigid or inflexible. Be careful that your "the rules apply to everybody" perspective doesn't keep you from considering legitimate exceptions and extenuating circumstances. Sometimes those with Consistency can come across as self-righteous, contending that their rules-based view of the world is the only way to view reality. You may tend to place undue emphasis on the way work gets done rather than focusing on the outcome — overvaluing process over results. You are so committed to treating each person the same that you might forget that no two people are alike. Remember that different things motivate different people, and everyone has their own way of thinking and relating to others. You may see yourself as a guardian of what is right and a champion against special treatment. Realize that others may reject you for assuming that responsibility and will want to bend the rules for the greater good.

gallup_top5.definition: “成就”才干主题突出的人大都兢兢业业，锲而不舍。 他们乐于忙忙碌碌并有所作为。
gallup_top5.feature: work hard and possess a great deal of stamina. They take immense satisfaction in being busy and productive.
gallup_top5.application: You likely have an innate intensity and drive that enables you to get a lot done and set the pace for your team. You are dependable — others can count on you to get the job done and reach your goals. Breaking a large project down into manageable tasks likely comes naturally to you. As such, you can help others see big jobs as less daunting. You have stamina — not only do you like to work hard, you derive a lot of energy from working hard; the harder you work, the more capacity you find you have.
gallup_top5.blind_spots: Your Achiever talents push you, so you might tend to push others — sometimes too hard. Because you work hard, you may become frustrated with those whom you perceive as not working as hard as you do; as such, peers may see you as too demanding, inflexible and judgmental of others. Your confidence in your ability to get things done may cause you to jump in and get started on a project before understanding all the details, nuances and demands of the project -- and that can get you into trouble. With your focus on task completion, you might overlook or underestimate the value of people and relationships. If you are a manager or team leader, you may need to remind yourself that managing is not only about getting work done through people, but as importantly it's about getting people done through work. People with strong Achiever talents may get frustrated when others don’t work as hard as they do. Others may see them as too demanding. They need to remember that not everyone has the same high expectations for themselves or is driven to work as hard as they are. Their pressing need to get things done might cause them to take on projects or agree to deadlines before they know everything that’s involved. Before they commit to something, high Achievers should make sure they have the time and resources they need to do it right.

gallup_top5.definition: “取悦”才干主题突出的人喜欢结交新人并博取其欢心。 他们能够在人际交往中与他人建立联系并从中得到满足。
gallup_top5.feature: love the challenge of meeting new people and winning them over. They derive satisfaction from breaking the ice and making a connection with someone.
gallup_top5.application: You are naturally friendly and quickly set people at ease. This makes others want to work with you. Your natural networking ability creates connections that you can share — when you talk with others, you often connect them with people who can help them. Your network is often a source of solutions when your team gets stuck and can't find the answer. Your bridge-building capability can be an asset in connecting your team with other departments and constituencies.
gallup_top5.blind_spots: Your need for making numerous connections can cause others to see you as superficial and less than genuine in your relationships. Check yourself — are you primarily making connections that will benefit you and your advancement, or are you genuinely making relationships that focus on others? People can see through insincerity. You want — need — to be liked, which by itself is not a bad thing. But sometimes this can prevent you from delivering hard news or having a necessary though difficult conversation. You are quick to praise others. Be sure your praise is authentic, deserved and individualised. Some people might find your high energy and natural enthusiasm off-putting. Take stock of your surroundings, and match your Woo to the needs of those you are winning over. Others may view you as shallow and insincere because you connect with people quickly then move on. In social situations, when you are talking with someone and have the urge to go meet someone else, try to remember that some people may just be getting comfortable. You may have a tendency to reveal yourself and trust others too quickly. This may cause more reserved people to step back from a relationship. Be aware that people’s personal comfort zones differ, and you will need to adjust your approach if you want to win people over.

gallup_top5.definition: “和谐”才干主题突出的人渴求协调一致。 他们避免冲突，寻求共识。
gallup_top5.feature: 和谐主题突出的人追求协调与共识，像和平天使一样化解冲突、营造和谐。
gallup_top5.application: 确保与周围人之间有开放、透明和及时的沟通渠道，避免信息不对称和误解，增强合作和和谐。 重视他人的观点和感受，积极倾听他人的想法和建议，展示出真诚的关怀和关心。 鼓励团队成员之间的合作和协作，强调团队的共同目标和利益，共同努力解决问题和实现目标。 处理冲突时采取积极的方法，鼓励开放的对话和理解，寻求妥协和共赢的解决方案，以维护和谐的关 系。
gallup_top5.blind_spots: · 合理的冲突可以带来创新和改进，过度追求和谐可能忽视了冲突的积极作用。 · 可能导致忽视个人的需求和边界，无法有效地满足自己的利益和利益。 · 压抑自己的情绪和需求，可能使得问题无法得到解决和满足。

gallup_top5.definition: “理念”才干主题突出的人痴迷于各种奇思妙想。 他们能够从看似毫无关联的现象中找到彼此之间的联系。
gallup_top5.feature: 他们对各种理念着迷，能够从看似毫无关联的现象中发现它们之间的相互关系。
gallup_top5.application: 花时间思考和定义自己的核心价值观和人生目标。这将帮助您在工作和生活中保持一致性，并使决策更 加有针对性和有意义。 寻找与自己价值观和理念相符的工作，或者在现有工作中找到与之相关的方面。将自己的使命融入到工 作中，让工作成为实现个人理念的途径。 与他人分享您的观点和见解，通过演讲、写作、教育等方式传播您的理念，争取更大的影响力。 如果您是团队的一员或领导者，努力营造一个共同理念和目标的团队文化，激发团队的凝聚力和合作精 神。
gallup_top5.blind_spots: · 陷入过于理想化的情境和理论框架中，而忽视现实的限制和挑战。可能追求完美的理念，但忽略了实际可行 性和可实施性。 · 如果倾向于简化和抽象化理念，而忽视了其中的多元性和复杂性，这种简化可能导致理念的不完整性和误导 性。 · 忽视不同文化和价值观之间的差异，将自己的理念强加于他人，导致理念的冲突和难以接受。

gallup_top5.definition: “战略”才干主题突出的人足智多谋。任何情形下， 他们都能迅速找出相关的规律和要点。
gallup_top5.feature: 战略主题突出的人具备卓越的智慧和谋略，能快速识别各种方案的模式和结果。
gallup_top5.application: 制定长期和短期的目标，并确保它们与您的价值观和职业发展路径相符，以实现自己的愿景。 运用战略思维和分析能力，评估当前的工作和生活状况，找到达成目标的最佳途径。 基于您的目标和分析结果，制定战略计划，并为每个步骤制定时间表和资源分配。 执行战略需要坚定的决心和毅力，保持积极的心态和专注力，持之以恒地执行战略计划。
gallup_top5.blind_spots: · 未能及时感知和适应外部环境的变化，导致战略的失效和跟不上市场的发展。 · 过度自信导致高估自身实力和低估竞争对手，容易陷入盲目行动和错误判断。 · 忽视竞争对手的动态和策略，无法有效应对竞争挑战和制定有效对策。

成就 / Achiever（执行力 / Executing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“成就”才干主题突出的人大都兢兢业业，锲而不舍。 他们乐于忙忙碌碌并有所作为。

特征 / Feature：work hard and possess a great deal of stamina. They take immense satisfaction in being busy and productive.

应用 / Application：You likely have an innate intensity and drive that enables you to get a lot done and set the pace for your team. You are dependable — others can count on you to get the job done and reach your goals. Breaking a large project down into manageable tasks likely comes naturally to you. As such, you can help others see big jobs as less daunting. You have stamina — not only do you like to work hard, you derive a lot of energy from working hard; the harder you work, the more capacity you find you have.

盲点 / Blind Spots：Your Achiever talents push you, so you might tend to push others — sometimes too hard. Because you work hard, you may become frustrated with those whom you perceive as not working as hard as you do; as such, peers may see you as too demanding, inflexible and judgmental of others. Your confidence in your ability to get things done may cause you to jump in and get started on a project before understanding all the details, nuances and demands of the project -- and that can get you into trouble. With your focus on task completion, you might overlook or underestimate the value of people and relationships. If you are a manager or team leader, you may need to remind yourself that managing is not only about getting work done through people, but as importantly it's about getting people done through work. People with strong Achiever talents may get frustrated when others don’t work as hard as they do. Others may see them as too demanding. They need to remember that not everyone has the same high expectations for themselves or is driven to work as hard as they are. Their pressing need to get things done might cause them to take on projects or agree to deadlines before they know everything that’s involved. Before they commit to something, high Achievers should make sure they have the time and resources they need to do it right.

取悦 / Woo（影响力 / Influencing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“取悦”才干主题突出的人喜欢结交新人并博取其欢心。 他们能够在人际交往中与他人建立联系并从中得到满足。

特征 / Feature：love the challenge of meeting new people and winning them over. They derive satisfaction from breaking the ice and making a connection with someone.

应用 / Application：You are naturally friendly and quickly set people at ease. This makes others want to work with you. Your natural networking ability creates connections that you can share — when you talk with others, you often connect them with people who can help them. Your network is often a source of solutions when your team gets stuck and can't find the answer. Your bridge-building capability can be an asset in connecting your team with other departments and constituencies.

盲点 / Blind Spots：Your need for making numerous connections can cause others to see you as superficial and less than genuine in your relationships. Check yourself — are you primarily making connections that will benefit you and your advancement, or are you genuinely making relationships that focus on others? People can see through insincerity. You want — need — to be liked, which by itself is not a bad thing. But sometimes this can prevent you from delivering hard news or having a necessary though difficult conversation. You are quick to praise others. Be sure your praise is authentic, deserved and individualised. Some people might find your high energy and natural enthusiasm off-putting. Take stock of your surroundings, and match your Woo to the needs of those you are winning over. Others may view you as shallow and insincere because you connect with people quickly then move on. In social situations, when you are talking with someone and have the urge to go meet someone else, try to remember that some people may just be getting comfortable. You may have a tendency to reveal yourself and trust others too quickly. This may cause more reserved people to step back from a relationship. Be aware that people’s personal comfort zones differ, and you will need to adjust your approach if you want to win people over.

和谐 / Harmony（关系建立 / Relationship Building）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“和谐”才干主题突出的人渴求协调一致。 他们避免冲突，寻求共识。

特征 / Feature：和谐主题突出的人追求协调与共识，像和平天使一样化解冲突、营造和谐。

应用 / Application：确保与周围人之间有开放、透明和及时的沟通渠道，避免信息不对称和误解，增强合作和和谐。 重视他人的观点和感受，积极倾听他人的想法和建议，展示出真诚的关怀和关心。 鼓励团队成员之间的合作和协作，强调团队的共同目标和利益，共同努力解决问题和实现目标。 处理冲突时采取积极的方法，鼓励开放的对话和理解，寻求妥协和共赢的解决方案，以维护和谐的关 系。

盲点 / Blind Spots：· 合理的冲突可以带来创新和改进，过度追求和谐可能忽视了冲突的积极作用。 · 可能导致忽视个人的需求和边界，无法有效地满足自己的利益和利益。 · 压抑自己的情绪和需求，可能使得问题无法得到解决和满足。

理念 / Ideation（战略思维 / Strategic Thinking）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“理念”才干主题突出的人痴迷于各种奇思妙想。 他们能够从看似毫无关联的现象中找到彼此之间的联系。

特征 / Feature：他们对各种理念着迷，能够从看似毫无关联的现象中发现它们之间的相互关系。

应用 / Application：花时间思考和定义自己的核心价值观和人生目标。这将帮助您在工作和生活中保持一致性，并使决策更 加有针对性和有意义。 寻找与自己价值观和理念相符的工作，或者在现有工作中找到与之相关的方面。将自己的使命融入到工 作中，让工作成为实现个人理念的途径。 与他人分享您的观点和见解，通过演讲、写作、教育等方式传播您的理念，争取更大的影响力。 如果您是团队的一员或领导者，努力营造一个共同理念和目标的团队文化，激发团队的凝聚力和合作精 神。

盲点 / Blind Spots：· 陷入过于理想化的情境和理论框架中，而忽视现实的限制和挑战。可能追求完美的理念，但忽略了实际可行 性和可实施性。 · 如果倾向于简化和抽象化理念，而忽视了其中的多元性和复杂性，这种简化可能导致理念的不完整性和误导 性。 · 忽视不同文化和价值观之间的差异，将自己的理念强加于他人，导致理念的冲突和难以接受。

战略 / Strategic（战略思维 / Strategic Thinking）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“战略”才干主题突出的人足智多谋。任何情形下， 他们都能迅速找出相关的规律和要点。

特征 / Feature：战略主题突出的人具备卓越的智慧和谋略，能快速识别各种方案的模式和结果。

应用 / Application：制定长期和短期的目标，并确保它们与您的价值观和职业发展路径相符，以实现自己的愿景。 运用战略思维和分析能力，评估当前的工作和生活状况，找到达成目标的最佳途径。 基于您的目标和分析结果，制定战略计划，并为每个步骤制定时间表和资源分配。 执行战略需要坚定的决心和毅力，保持积极的心态和专注力，持之以恒地执行战略计划。

盲点 / Blind Spots：· 未能及时感知和适应外部环境的变化，导致战略的失效和跟不上市场的发展。 · 过度自信导致高估自身实力和低估竞争对手，容易陷入盲目行动和错误判断。 · 忽视竞争对手的动态和策略，无法有效应对竞争挑战和制定有效对策。

gallup_top5.definition: 沟通能力强的人善于用言辞表达他们的想法。 他们善于与人交谈，擅长讲解事物。
gallup_top5.feature: generally find it easy to put their thoughts into words. They are good conversationalists and presenters.
gallup_top5.application: You have the ability to bring attention to important messages. When you connect a story to an issue, you make everyone in the room smarter. You can generate dialogue and build consensus in team settings. You likely excel at summarising information that can help teams find common ground. You naturally look for ways to fine-tune and maximise your message, creating energy, acceptance and understanding. You have a natural talent for putting your emotions into words and you can help others find the right words to express what they are feeling.
gallup_top5.blind_spots: Because you are naturally verbal and instinctively share your thoughts, you may not realize when you are monopolising a discussion. Try to be mindful of when other people have something to say, and consider pausing so they can say it. Even though talking comes easily to you, remember that you don't have to be the one to convey the message. Use your ability to find the right words to empower others to communicate better and share their thoughts and ideas. Great communication skills don't involve just talking — they also involve listening. Remind yourself to listen, reflect and encourage dialogue. Take time to listen and pay attention to what others are saying — and not saying. Your ease with words and descriptions can influence how people perceive others. Be careful what you say about others -- be positive and encouraging, as your words carry weight. Because you are naturally verbal and instinctively share your thoughts, you may not realize when you are monopolising a discussion. Try to be mindful of when other people have something to say, and consider pausing so they can say it. Remember that your words are not always enough to motivate others. Take time to listen and pay attention to what others are saying — and not saying.

gallup_top5.definition: 竞争性强的人参照他人的表现来衡量自身的进步。 他们力争第一，陶醉于竞争的喜悦中。
gallup_top5.feature: measure their progress against the performance of others. They strive to win first place and revel in contests.
gallup_top5.application: You create daily, monthly and yearly metrics for success to track your performance scores. This creates an energy for your team that pushes them to reach higher levels of achievement. Your drive to outperform others can stimulate innovation and creativity. You are naturally attuned to real-world measures that assess achievement, leading you to describe what outstanding performance looks like. Your drive to win and your need for clear metrics that define success can inspire others and cause them to want to be on your team.
gallup_top5.blind_spots: If you lead a team, competing to win against your team is counter-productive. Instead, find a way to use your Competition to define and celebrate team wins. Your Competition talents push you, so you might tend to push others — sometimes too hard. Remember that winning at all costs isn't winning; it's defeating yourself. Make sure your integrity remains intact when you push yourself and your team to victory. Don't let your competitive spirit cloud your relationships with your team members and peers. Find ways to unite your team members by finding what each does best and positioning them for maximum effectiveness to create a winning combination. Be careful not to let your competitive spirit get in the way of your relationships. Not everyone has your drive to win, and constantly comparing your performance to theirs might offend them or hurt their feelings. Winning at all costs isn’t winning; it’s self-defeating. Make sure you maintain your principles when you push yourself toward ultimate victory.

gallup_top5.definition: “成就”才干主题突出的人大都兢兢业业，锲而不舍。 他们乐于忙忙碌碌并有所作为。
gallup_top5.feature: work hard and possess a great deal of stamina. They take immense satisfaction in being busy and productive.
gallup_top5.application: You likely have an innate intensity and drive that enables you to get a lot done and set the pace for your team. You are dependable — others can count on you to get the job done and reach your goals. Breaking a large project down into manageable tasks likely comes naturally to you. As such, you can help others see big jobs as less daunting. You have stamina — not only do you like to work hard, you derive a lot of energy from working hard; the harder you work, the more capacity you find you have.
gallup_top5.blind_spots: Your Achiever talents push you, so you might tend to push others — sometimes too hard. Because you work hard, you may become frustrated with those whom you perceive as not working as hard as you do; as such, peers may see you as too demanding, inflexible and judgmental of others. Your confidence in your ability to get things done may cause you to jump in and get started on a project before understanding all the details, nuances and demands of the project -- and that can get you into trouble. With your focus on task completion, you might overlook or underestimate the value of people and relationships. If you are a manager or team leader, you may need to remind yourself that managing is not only about getting work done through people, but as importantly it's about getting people done through work. People with strong Achiever talents may get frustrated when others don’t work as hard as they do. Others may see them as too demanding. They need to remember that not everyone has the same high expectations for themselves or is driven to work as hard as they are. Their pressing need to get things done might cause them to take on projects or agree to deadlines before they know everything that’s involved. Before they commit to something, high Achievers should make sure they have the time and resources they need to do it right.

gallup_top5.blind_spots: · 陷入过于理想化的情境和理论框架中，而忽视现实的限制和挑战。可能追求完美的理念，但忽略了实际可行 性和可实施性。 · 如果倾向于简化和抽象化理念，而忽视了其中的多元性和复杂性，这种简化可能导致理念的不完整性和误导 性。 · 忽视不同文化和价值观之间的差异，将自己的理念强加于他人，导致理念的冲突和难以接受。

gallup_top5.definition: 有强烈信仰的人拥有某种永恒不变的核心价值观。 并由此形成明确的生活目标。
gallup_top5.feature: have certain core values that are unchanging. Out of these values emerges a defined purpose for their lives.
gallup_top5.application: You can be a conscience for your team and organization; you provide stability by clearly stating and demonstrating organisational values. You are dependable — others know where you stand and can count on your moral compass. In times of change, you can provide team and organisational stability by communicating core values that should not and will not change. Because your work matters to you, you can help others see the intrinsic value of their work and the valuable contributions they make to the team's and organization's mission.
gallup_top5.blind_spots: Your Belief talents make you strong and confident, but if you do not honour the beliefs of those on your team you may find yourself working alone. Because your work must matter, you may have a hard time understanding and relating to those for whom work is primarily a paycheck. Their income might be the vehicle by which they fulfil their meaning and purpose outside of the workplace. Beware of snap judgments. If others' values don't align with yours, you can tend to be judgmental when evaluating their motivations. For you, mission is primary, which is admirable but sometimes seen as impractical -- especially when it comes to budgetary constraints. Remember that organisations must be financially viable to fulfil their mission. Because you are passionate about your own beliefs, others might view you as stubborn or set in your ways. Keep in mind that everyone has a unique worldview, and often, it will not be the same as yours. Be careful not to pass judgment on others’ principles and motivations. Their values and belief systems deserve your respect, even when you don’t agree with them.

沟通 / Communication（影响力 / Influencing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：沟通能力强的人善于用言辞表达他们的想法。 他们善于与人交谈，擅长讲解事物。

特征 / Feature：generally find it easy to put their thoughts into words. They are good conversationalists and presenters.

应用 / Application：You have the ability to bring attention to important messages. When you connect a story to an issue, you make everyone in the room smarter. You can generate dialogue and build consensus in team settings. You likely excel at summarising information that can help teams find common ground. You naturally look for ways to fine-tune and maximise your message, creating energy, acceptance and understanding. You have a natural talent for putting your emotions into words and you can help others find the right words to express what they are feeling.

盲点 / Blind Spots：Because you are naturally verbal and instinctively share your thoughts, you may not realize when you are monopolising a discussion. Try to be mindful of when other people have something to say, and consider pausing so they can say it. Even though talking comes easily to you, remember that you don't have to be the one to convey the message. Use your ability to find the right words to empower others to communicate better and share their thoughts and ideas. Great communication skills don't involve just talking — they also involve listening. Remind yourself to listen, reflect and encourage dialogue. Take time to listen and pay attention to what others are saying — and not saying. Your ease with words and descriptions can influence how people perceive others. Be careful what you say about others -- be positive and encouraging, as your words carry weight. Because you are naturally verbal and instinctively share your thoughts, you may not realize when you are monopolising a discussion. Try to be mindful of when other people have something to say, and consider pausing so they can say it. Remember that your words are not always enough to motivate others. Take time to listen and pay attention to what others are saying — and not saying.

竞争 / Competition（影响力 / Influencing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：竞争性强的人参照他人的表现来衡量自身的进步。 他们力争第一，陶醉于竞争的喜悦中。

特征 / Feature：measure their progress against the performance of others. They strive to win first place and revel in contests.

应用 / Application：You create daily, monthly and yearly metrics for success to track your performance scores. This creates an energy for your team that pushes them to reach higher levels of achievement. Your drive to outperform others can stimulate innovation and creativity. You are naturally attuned to real-world measures that assess achievement, leading you to describe what outstanding performance looks like. Your drive to win and your need for clear metrics that define success can inspire others and cause them to want to be on your team.

盲点 / Blind Spots：If you lead a team, competing to win against your team is counter-productive. Instead, find a way to use your Competition to define and celebrate team wins. Your Competition talents push you, so you might tend to push others — sometimes too hard. Remember that winning at all costs isn't winning; it's defeating yourself. Make sure your integrity remains intact when you push yourself and your team to victory. Don't let your competitive spirit cloud your relationships with your team members and peers. Find ways to unite your team members by finding what each does best and positioning them for maximum effectiveness to create a winning combination. Be careful not to let your competitive spirit get in the way of your relationships. Not everyone has your drive to win, and constantly comparing your performance to theirs might offend them or hurt their feelings. Winning at all costs isn’t winning; it’s self-defeating. Make sure you maintain your principles when you push yourself toward ultimate victory.

成就 / Achiever（执行力 / Executing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“成就”才干主题突出的人大都兢兢业业，锲而不舍。 他们乐于忙忙碌碌并有所作为。

特征 / Feature：work hard and possess a great deal of stamina. They take immense satisfaction in being busy and productive.

应用 / Application：You likely have an innate intensity and drive that enables you to get a lot done and set the pace for your team. You are dependable — others can count on you to get the job done and reach your goals. Breaking a large project down into manageable tasks likely comes naturally to you. As such, you can help others see big jobs as less daunting. You have stamina — not only do you like to work hard, you derive a lot of energy from working hard; the harder you work, the more capacity you find you have.

盲点 / Blind Spots：Your Achiever talents push you, so you might tend to push others — sometimes too hard. Because you work hard, you may become frustrated with those whom you perceive as not working as hard as you do; as such, peers may see you as too demanding, inflexible and judgmental of others. Your confidence in your ability to get things done may cause you to jump in and get started on a project before understanding all the details, nuances and demands of the project -- and that can get you into trouble. With your focus on task completion, you might overlook or underestimate the value of people and relationships. If you are a manager or team leader, you may need to remind yourself that managing is not only about getting work done through people, but as importantly it's about getting people done through work. People with strong Achiever talents may get frustrated when others don’t work as hard as they do. Others may see them as too demanding. They need to remember that not everyone has the same high expectations for themselves or is driven to work as hard as they are. Their pressing need to get things done might cause them to take on projects or agree to deadlines before they know everything that’s involved. Before they commit to something, high Achievers should make sure they have the time and resources they need to do it right.

理念 / Ideation（战略思维 / Strategic Thinking）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“理念”才干主题突出的人痴迷于各种奇思妙想。 他们能够从看似毫无关联的现象中找到彼此之间的联系。

特征 / Feature：他们对各种理念着迷，能够从看似毫无关联的现象中发现它们之间的相互关系。

应用 / Application：花时间思考和定义自己的核心价值观和人生目标。这将帮助您在工作和生活中保持一致性，并使决策更 加有针对性和有意义。 寻找与自己价值观和理念相符的工作，或者在现有工作中找到与之相关的方面。将自己的使命融入到工 作中，让工作成为实现个人理念的途径。 与他人分享您的观点和见解，通过演讲、写作、教育等方式传播您的理念，争取更大的影响力。 如果您是团队的一员或领导者，努力营造一个共同理念和目标的团队文化，激发团队的凝聚力和合作精 神。

盲点 / Blind Spots：· 陷入过于理想化的情境和理论框架中，而忽视现实的限制和挑战。可能追求完美的理念，但忽略了实际可行 性和可实施性。 · 如果倾向于简化和抽象化理念，而忽视了其中的多元性和复杂性，这种简化可能导致理念的不完整性和误导 性。 · 忽视不同文化和价值观之间的差异，将自己的理念强加于他人，导致理念的冲突和难以接受。

信仰 / Belief（执行力 / Executing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：有强烈信仰的人拥有某种永恒不变的核心价值观。 并由此形成明确的生活目标。

特征 / Feature：have certain core values that are unchanging. Out of these values emerges a defined purpose for their lives.

应用 / Application：You can be a conscience for your team and organization; you provide stability by clearly stating and demonstrating organisational values. You are dependable — others know where you stand and can count on your moral compass. In times of change, you can provide team and organisational stability by communicating core values that should not and will not change. Because your work matters to you, you can help others see the intrinsic value of their work and the valuable contributions they make to the team's and organization's mission.

盲点 / Blind Spots：Your Belief talents make you strong and confident, but if you do not honour the beliefs of those on your team you may find yourself working alone. Because your work must matter, you may have a hard time understanding and relating to those for whom work is primarily a paycheck. Their income might be the vehicle by which they fulfil their meaning and purpose outside of the workplace. Beware of snap judgments. If others' values don't align with yours, you can tend to be judgmental when evaluating their motivations. For you, mission is primary, which is admirable but sometimes seen as impractical -- especially when it comes to budgetary constraints. Remember that organisations must be financially viable to fulfil their mission. Because you are passionate about your own beliefs, others might view you as stubborn or set in your ways. Keep in mind that everyone has a unique worldview, and often, it will not be the same as yours. Be careful not to pass judgment on others’ principles and motivations. Their values and belief systems deserve your respect, even when you don’t agree with them.

成就 / Achiever（执行力 / Executing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“成就”才干主题突出的人大都兢兢业业，锲而不舍。 他们乐于忙忙碌碌并有所作为。

特征 / Feature：work hard and possess a great deal of stamina. They take immense satisfaction in being busy and productive.

应用 / Application：You likely have an innate intensity and drive that enables you to get a lot done and set the pace for your team. You are dependable — others can count on you to get the job done and reach your goals. Breaking a large project down into manageable tasks likely comes naturally to you. As such, you can help others see big jobs as less daunting. You have stamina — not only do you like to work hard, you derive a lot of energy from working hard; the harder you work, the more capacity you find you have.

盲点 / Blind Spots：Your Achiever talents push you, so you might tend to push others — sometimes too hard. Because you work hard, you may become frustrated with those whom you perceive as not working as hard as you do; as such, peers may see you as too demanding, inflexible and judgmental of others. Your confidence in your ability to get things done may cause you to jump in and get started on a project before understanding all the details, nuances and demands of the project -- and that can get you into trouble. With your focus on task completion, you might overlook or underestimate the value of people and relationships. If you are a manager or team leader, you may need to remind yourself that managing is not only about getting work done through people, but as importantly it's about getting people done through work. People with strong Achiever talents may get frustrated when others don’t work as hard as they do. Others may see them as too demanding. They need to remember that not everyone has the same high expectations for themselves or is driven to work as hard as they are. Their pressing need to get things done might cause them to take on projects or agree to deadlines before they know everything that’s involved. Before they commit to something, high Achievers should make sure they have the time and resources they need to do it right.

排难 / Restorative（执行力 / Executing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“排难”才干主题突出的人是解决问题的行家里手。 他们善于发现问题并解决问题。

特征 / Feature：are adept at dealing with problems. They are good at figuring out what is wrong and resolving it.

应用 / Application：You have a unique ability to look past surface-level symptoms to find the root causes of problems — your solutions are not stopgap, but rather are intended to last. You are a troubleshooter, and in that role, you increase efficiency and reach the goal sooner. Your clear-eyed analysis makes you extremely valuable in crises. You close the loop, restore order and clean up the mess — and enjoy the process. Your Restorative talents lend themselves well to continuous improvement processes. You find what is wrong, determine how it can be fixed and take things to the next level.

盲点 / Blind Spots：Your focus on finding problems could bring you a reputation for being negative — especially when you find problems that others haven't considered, let alone seen. Help your team members realise you aren't wanting to "rain on their parade," but rather anticipate and avoid problems that would prevent the parade from even occurring. You are energised by examining problems and delving into thorny, complex situations. You may even feel a bit let down when the problem is solved. Because this gives you energy, be aware that you might feel a need to create problems to solve where none actually exist. Depending on the nature of your Restorative talents, you may focus on processes and not people. Give praise where it is due, and don't overly focus on shortcomings — yours or others'. You may tend to jump in and rescue others when they are dealing with a problem. Be discerning, and resist the urge to solve others' problems for them. More often than not, we learn the most from the problems we face and solve ourselves. Others might think that all you see are their flaws and shortcomings. Remember that there are times when people need you to see their successes and give them praise. You tend to focus on processes, not people, and you might automatically rush to solve every problem. Sometimes others need to solve their own problems, so try to give people space to experiment and find their own solutions.

伯乐 / Developer（关系建立 / Relationship Building）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“伯乐”才干主题突出者善于发现并培养他人的潜能。 他们能够察觉任何微小的进步，并从中得到满足感。

特征 / Feature：欣赏他人潜力并善于发现进步的人，喜悦于细微的成长，激励他人不断进步。

应用 / Application：观察和评估身边的人，发现他们的优点、才能和潜力，注意他们的成就和特长。 与他人建立良好的沟通和合作关系，了解他们的目标和需求，帮助他们发展他们的潜力，并在实现目标 的过程中提供指导和建议。 通过认可和赞赏，激发他人的动力和自信心。表达对他们的信任，并鼓励他们尝试新的挑战和机会。 通过良好的领导实践和积极的影响力，发展自己的领导能力，成为他人的榜样和引导者。 与他人建立导师关系，分享经验和知识，帮助他们发展专业能力和职业发展。

盲点 / Blind Spots：· 依赖他人的赞许和认可，忽视自身的价值和成就，导致自我价值感受受损。 · 过分专注于他人的潜力和成功，忽略了自己的个人成长和发展，可能导致自身进步的缓慢。 · 对他人的成长和进步寄予过高期望，忽视每个人的独特情况和发展速度，可能带来不必要的压力和失望。

学习 / Learner（战略思维 / Strategic Thinking）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：学习能力强的人有旺盛的求知欲，渴望不断提高自我。 令他们激动的是求知的过程而非结果。

特征 / Feature：学习能力强的人具备强烈求知欲望，对知识的获取过程更加激动人心，而非仅关注结果。

应用 / Application：将学到的知识和技能应用于工作和生活中，将学习转化为实际行动。找到机会将学习成果展示和分享， 为团队和组织带来积极影响。 保持好奇心和开放心态，积极主动地探索新的领域和知识。参与跨部门项目、交叉学科的讨论和合作， 拓宽自己的视野和经验。 掌握有效的学习方法和技巧，如提取关键信息、整理笔记、积极参与讨论、反思和总结等。寻找适合自 己的学习方式，提高学习效率和成果。

盲点 / Blind Spots：· 忽视学习和掌握基础知识的重要性，直接进入高级概念和技能，导致学习的薄弱环节和不牢固的基础。 · 选择不适合自己的学习方法，导致学习效率低下或学习成果不理想。 · 只停留在理论层面，不进行实践和应用，无法将所学知识转化为实际能力和技能。

竞争 / Competition（影响力 / Influencing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：竞争性强的人参照他人的表现来衡量自身的进步。 他们力争第一，陶醉于竞争的喜悦中。

特征 / Feature：measure their progress against the performance of others. They strive to win first place and revel in contests.

应用 / Application：You create daily, monthly and yearly metrics for success to track your performance scores. This creates an energy for your team that pushes them to reach higher levels of achievement. Your drive to outperform others can stimulate innovation and creativity. You are naturally attuned to real-world measures that assess achievement, leading you to describe what outstanding performance looks like. Your drive to win and your need for clear metrics that define success can inspire others and cause them to want to be on your team.

盲点 / Blind Spots：If you lead a team, competing to win against your team is counter-productive. Instead, find a way to use your Competition to define and celebrate team wins. Your Competition talents push you, so you might tend to push others — sometimes too hard. Remember that winning at all costs isn't winning; it's defeating yourself. Make sure your integrity remains intact when you push yourself and your team to victory. Don't let your competitive spirit cloud your relationships with your team members and peers. Find ways to unite your team members by finding what each does best and positioning them for maximum effectiveness to create a winning combination. Be careful not to let your competitive spirit get in the way of your relationships. Not everyone has your drive to win, and constantly comparing your performance to theirs might offend them or hurt their feelings. Winning at all costs isn’t winning; it’s self-defeating. Make sure you maintain your principles when you push yourself toward ultimate victory.

主导领域分析 / Leading Domain Analysis：Top 5 中 执行力 / Executing 领域占 2 项，说明该生在行动方式上偏向 实干完成型 / Efficient Executor。

In your Top 5, Executing accounts for 2 themes, indicating a natural tendency toward Efficient Executor.

gallup_top5.definition: “体谅”才干主题突出的人能够设身处地地体会他人的 感受。
gallup_top5.feature: 具备同理心的人是心灵导师，善于感同身受，体察他人情感，以温暖他人心灵。
gallup_top5.application: 积极倾听他人的意见和观点，尊重他人的感受和想法，表达出对他人感受的关心和共鸣。 尊重和接纳不同个体的差异，包括文化背景、观念和工作风格等，鼓励多样性和不同声音的表达。 关注他人的困难和挑战，愿意伸出援手并提供实质性的帮助，共同解决问题并实现共同目标。 关注并回应他人的需求。表达对他人的关心和支持，提供合适的帮助和资源，以满足他人的需求，并促 进他人的成长和发展。
gallup_top5.blind_spots: · 过度假设他人的感受可能导致误解和误判，无法真正理解他人的需求和情感。 · 自我牺牲和忽视自己的需求和边界，使自己处于不利的境地。 · 不敢表达自己的意见和需求，忽视了良好的沟通和建立互相理解的机会。

gallup_top5.definition: 交往能力强的人喜欢人际间的亲密关系。 他们最大的满足是与朋友一道为实现一个目标而共同努力。
gallup_top5.feature: 交往能力强的人享受亲密的人际关系。他们最大的乐趣是与朋友一起合作，共同实现共同目标。
gallup_top5.application: 愿意与他人分享知识和经验，支持他人的成长和发展。 尊重和接纳他人的不同观点、文化和背景。倡导多样性和包容性，并与不同背景的人合作，增进相互理 解和合作。 重视他人的观点和感受。倾听他人的需求和问题，并展现出真正的关心。 培养良好的社交技巧，学会察言观色，适应不同的人际交往风格，以更好地理解和与他人沟通。 培养解决问题的技巧，处理冲突时保持冷静和客观，寻求妥协和解决方案，有效沟通，寻求共同的利益 和理解。
gallup_top5.blind_spots: · 过于坚持己见和固定的交往模式，不愿意适应和调整自己的行为和观念，影响交往的互动性和流畅度。 · 忽视寻找共同的利益点和共同目标，导致交往关系的表面化和短暂性。 · 不尊重他人的个人空间和隐私，过于侵入性的行为和过问，造成他人的不适和疏离。

gallup_top5.definition: 统率力强的人有大将风度。他们运筹帷幄，指挥若定。
gallup_top5.feature: have presence. They can take control of a situation and make decisions.
gallup_top5.application: You have a natural ability to inspire and, as such, your words carry weight. Others often will defer to your personality as much as to your expertise or experience. While you may value time alone, you are not a "loner" — you need people; specifically, you need people to follow you. Because you are focused on others, you can be charming in your efforts to gain followers. This quality makes others want to follow your lead. You have a very protective nature — you will stand up and often intimidate those who would threaten your team at work or the people you love and care about. You do not hide from the truth, no matter how unpleasant that might be. This leads you to say out loud what others may only be thinking. You can become the voice of the voiceless.
gallup_top5.blind_spots: Your Command talents drive you to seek clarity, often through confrontation. Others can see this as intimidating and may even perceive you as a bully. Be careful not to intimidate those you seek to inspire. You are naturally decisive which can cause others to shy away from sharing their insights and ideas. Seek others' points of view before you make a decision. You have an opinion about the way things should be done and are not shy about expressing it. Be careful about imposing your will upon others lest they see you as bossy or arrogant. You are direct and sometimes can offend others without meaning to. Find ways to soften your approach and expand your influence — create an encouraging environment to draw others to follow you. Your decisiveness and presence can be intimidating, whether you intend them to be or not. Keep that in mind, especially when you have to coach others, deliver feedback and set expectations. Because you speak with authority, you might be used to getting the final word. Consider asking others for their input before sharing yours so they have a chance to contribute.

gallup_top5.definition: 适应力强的人倾向于“随大流”。他们活在“当前”， 接受现实，随遇而安。
gallup_top5.feature: 适应性强的人倾向于顺应潮流，他们活在当下，接受现实，随遇而安。
gallup_top5.application: 积极面对变化并适应新的情况 ,培养灵活性和应变能力，学会适应不断变化的工作环境和生活需求。 寻求灵活的工作安排，如弹性工作时间或远程办公，以更好地适应个人需求和工作要求。 与不同背景和经验的人合作，开阔自己的视野，学会从多个角度看待问题，以更好地适应多样化的工作 环境。
gallup_top5.blind_spots: · 过于顺应他人和环境的期待，而忽视了自己真实的需求和价值观。 · 太重视与他人的和谐相处，而忽视了自己的个人成长和发展的重要性。 · 对变化和不确定性感到不适应，导致焦虑和固执于既定的方式和习惯。

gallup_top5.definition: “成就”才干主题突出的人大都兢兢业业，锲而不舍。 他们乐于忙忙碌碌并有所作为。
gallup_top5.feature: work hard and possess a great deal of stamina. They take immense satisfaction in being busy and productive.
gallup_top5.application: You likely have an innate intensity and drive that enables you to get a lot done and set the pace for your team. You are dependable — others can count on you to get the job done and reach your goals. Breaking a large project down into manageable tasks likely comes naturally to you. As such, you can help others see big jobs as less daunting. You have stamina — not only do you like to work hard, you derive a lot of energy from working hard; the harder you work, the more capacity you find you have.
gallup_top5.blind_spots: Your Achiever talents push you, so you might tend to push others — sometimes too hard. Because you work hard, you may become frustrated with those whom you perceive as not working as hard as you do; as such, peers may see you as too demanding, inflexible and judgmental of others. Your confidence in your ability to get things done may cause you to jump in and get started on a project before understanding all the details, nuances and demands of the project -- and that can get you into trouble. With your focus on task completion, you might overlook or underestimate the value of people and relationships. If you are a manager or team leader, you may need to remind yourself that managing is not only about getting work done through people, but as importantly it's about getting people done through work. People with strong Achiever talents may get frustrated when others don’t work as hard as they do. Others may see them as too demanding. They need to remember that not everyone has the same high expectations for themselves or is driven to work as hard as they are. Their pressing need to get things done might cause them to take on projects or agree to deadlines before they know everything that’s involved. Before they commit to something, high Achievers should make sure they have the time and resources they need to do it right.

体谅 / Empathy（关系建立 / Relationship Building）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“体谅”才干主题突出的人能够设身处地地体会他人的 感受。

特征 / Feature：具备同理心的人是心灵导师，善于感同身受，体察他人情感，以温暖他人心灵。

应用 / Application：积极倾听他人的意见和观点，尊重他人的感受和想法，表达出对他人感受的关心和共鸣。 尊重和接纳不同个体的差异，包括文化背景、观念和工作风格等，鼓励多样性和不同声音的表达。 关注他人的困难和挑战，愿意伸出援手并提供实质性的帮助，共同解决问题并实现共同目标。 关注并回应他人的需求。表达对他人的关心和支持，提供合适的帮助和资源，以满足他人的需求，并促 进他人的成长和发展。

盲点 / Blind Spots：· 过度假设他人的感受可能导致误解和误判，无法真正理解他人的需求和情感。 · 自我牺牲和忽视自己的需求和边界，使自己处于不利的境地。 · 不敢表达自己的意见和需求，忽视了良好的沟通和建立互相理解的机会。

交往 / Relator（关系建立 / Relationship Building）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：交往能力强的人喜欢人际间的亲密关系。 他们最大的满足是与朋友一道为实现一个目标而共同努力。

特征 / Feature：交往能力强的人享受亲密的人际关系。他们最大的乐趣是与朋友一起合作，共同实现共同目标。

应用 / Application：愿意与他人分享知识和经验，支持他人的成长和发展。 尊重和接纳他人的不同观点、文化和背景。倡导多样性和包容性，并与不同背景的人合作，增进相互理 解和合作。 重视他人的观点和感受。倾听他人的需求和问题，并展现出真正的关心。 培养良好的社交技巧，学会察言观色，适应不同的人际交往风格，以更好地理解和与他人沟通。 培养解决问题的技巧，处理冲突时保持冷静和客观，寻求妥协和解决方案，有效沟通，寻求共同的利益 和理解。

盲点 / Blind Spots：· 过于坚持己见和固定的交往模式，不愿意适应和调整自己的行为和观念，影响交往的互动性和流畅度。 · 忽视寻找共同的利益点和共同目标，导致交往关系的表面化和短暂性。 · 不尊重他人的个人空间和隐私，过于侵入性的行为和过问，造成他人的不适和疏离。

统率 / Command（影响力 / Influencing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：统率力强的人有大将风度。他们运筹帷幄，指挥若定。

特征 / Feature：have presence. They can take control of a situation and make decisions.

应用 / Application：You have a natural ability to inspire and, as such, your words carry weight. Others often will defer to your personality as much as to your expertise or experience. While you may value time alone, you are not a "loner" — you need people; specifically, you need people to follow you. Because you are focused on others, you can be charming in your efforts to gain followers. This quality makes others want to follow your lead. You have a very protective nature — you will stand up and often intimidate those who would threaten your team at work or the people you love and care about. You do not hide from the truth, no matter how unpleasant that might be. This leads you to say out loud what others may only be thinking. You can become the voice of the voiceless.

盲点 / Blind Spots：Your Command talents drive you to seek clarity, often through confrontation. Others can see this as intimidating and may even perceive you as a bully. Be careful not to intimidate those you seek to inspire. You are naturally decisive which can cause others to shy away from sharing their insights and ideas. Seek others' points of view before you make a decision. You have an opinion about the way things should be done and are not shy about expressing it. Be careful about imposing your will upon others lest they see you as bossy or arrogant. You are direct and sometimes can offend others without meaning to. Find ways to soften your approach and expand your influence — create an encouraging environment to draw others to follow you. Your decisiveness and presence can be intimidating, whether you intend them to be or not. Keep that in mind, especially when you have to coach others, deliver feedback and set expectations. Because you speak with authority, you might be used to getting the final word. Consider asking others for their input before sharing yours so they have a chance to contribute.

适应 / Adaptability（关系建立 / Relationship Building）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：适应力强的人倾向于“随大流”。他们活在“当前”， 接受现实，随遇而安。

特征 / Feature：适应性强的人倾向于顺应潮流，他们活在当下，接受现实，随遇而安。

应用 / Application：积极面对变化并适应新的情况 ,培养灵活性和应变能力，学会适应不断变化的工作环境和生活需求。 寻求灵活的工作安排，如弹性工作时间或远程办公，以更好地适应个人需求和工作要求。 与不同背景和经验的人合作，开阔自己的视野，学会从多个角度看待问题，以更好地适应多样化的工作 环境。

盲点 / Blind Spots：· 过于顺应他人和环境的期待，而忽视了自己真实的需求和价值观。 · 太重视与他人的和谐相处，而忽视了自己的个人成长和发展的重要性。 · 对变化和不确定性感到不适应，导致焦虑和固执于既定的方式和习惯。

成就 / Achiever（执行力 / Executing）

标准定义 / Standard Definition：“成就”才干主题突出的人大都兢兢业业，锲而不舍。 他们乐于忙忙碌碌并有所作为。

特征 / Feature：work hard and possess a great deal of stamina. They take immense satisfaction in being busy and productive.

应用 / Application：You likely have an innate intensity and drive that enables you to get a lot done and set the pace for your team. You are dependable — others can count on you to get the job done and reach your goals. Breaking a large project down into manageable tasks likely comes naturally to you. As such, you can help others see big jobs as less daunting. You have stamina — not only do you like to work hard, you derive a lot of energy from working hard; the harder you work, the more capacity you find you have.

盲点 / Blind Spots：Your Achiever talents push you, so you might tend to push others — sometimes too hard. Because you work hard, you may become frustrated with those whom you perceive as not working as hard as you do; as such, peers may see you as too demanding, inflexible and judgmental of others. Your confidence in your ability to get things done may cause you to jump in and get started on a project before understanding all the details, nuances and demands of the project -- and that can get you into trouble. With your focus on task completion, you might overlook or underestimate the value of people and relationships. If you are a manager or team leader, you may need to remind yourself that managing is not only about getting work done through people, but as importantly it's about getting people done through work. People with strong Achiever talents may get frustrated when others don’t work as hard as they do. Others may see them as too demanding. They need to remember that not everyone has the same high expectations for themselves or is driven to work as hard as they are. Their pressing need to get things done might cause them to take on projects or agree to deadlines before they know everything that’s involved. Before they commit to something, high Achievers should make sure they have the time and resources they need to do it right.
